reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND in-page in headless Chrome — real geometrically-consistent correspondences; retrieve+verify multi-model verified; desktop+mobile layout checked; page-load conformance clean), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (raw ONNX Runtime Web 1.21.0, WASM EP, fresh profile). Standalone: ALIKED (aliked-n16rot-top1k-640.onnx, input 'image' [1,3,640,640]) returned keypoints [1000,2] (normalised), descriptors [1000,128], scores [1000]; LightGlue (lightglue_for_aliked.onnx, kpts0/1 [1,N,2] + desc0/1 [1,N,128]) returned matches0 [M,2] + mscores0. On an image vs itself shifted -50px in a 640px frame it produced 925 matches whose MEDIAN displacement was -0.1565 in normalised units = exactly -50/320 -> the matched keypoints land on the SAME physical points (real, geometrically consistent, NOT random). In-page (CDP driver, fresh profile -> Download ~51 MB -> auto-init -> Match): a scene vs its rotated(12deg)+scaled(0.9x) copy gave 785 correspondences, median confidence 0.996, 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Both ONNX graphs + the 640x640 resize preprocessing run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; ImageBitmaps are transferred in. transformers.js exposes no matcher class, so the graphs run directly via an isolated per-worker onnxruntime-web pin (precedent: yolov8-pose).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = per-image keypoints + descriptors, and across the pair a set of matched index pairs with confidences. The worker maps normalised keypoints back to each image's pixel space and returns {pairs:[{a,b,score}], keypointsA/B}. Semantic sanity confirmed: median displacement exactly matched the known shift; the drawn lines connect corresponding structure across the two photos.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT capability from the pose/landmark demos: two-image local-feature MATCHING (ALIKED detector/descriptor + LightGlue learned matcher), not single-image keypoints.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact repo (bukuroo/ALIKED-LightGlue-ONNX, linked), the two models (ALIKED-N16rot top-1000 640px + LightGlue), the task (keypoint-detection + matching), the pipeline (extract per image -> match descriptor sets), output shapes, quantisation (fp32), runtime (onnxruntime-web, because no transformers.js matcher class exists), backend (WASM), size (~51 MB total), and licenses (ALIKED BSD-3, LightGlue Apache-2.0).
rubric.notes: Inference is off the main thread; the 'How the API works' snippet shows the real two-session flow with the batch-dim glue LightGlue needs.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'bukuroo/ALIKED-LightGlue-ONNX', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:51}); the worker fetches BOTH ONNX files THROUGH Cache Storage under the '/bukuroo/ALIKED-LightGlue-ONNX/' path so lib/model-cache.js scans them -> auto-init on a returning visit, honest Download on first visit, per-model clear-cache.
rubric.notes: Verified: fresh profile -> Download (~51 MB, combined progress across both files) -> auto-init ready in the CDP driver.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/matching status is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes Retry on failure; an Extracting.../N correspondences status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Matching failed: ...').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: sample scene thumbnails (role=button + keyboard), independent Upload A / Upload B, rotation/scale/min-confidence sliders, Match button, show-all-keypoints and stack-vertically checkboxes. Ladder adds a duplicate-detector dual-upload + presets (practical), align sliders (wild), and a gallery + query selector (multi-model).
rubric.notes: Ladder: basics (match under a known transform), practical (same-scene/duplicate detector with a verdict + RANSAC inliers), wild (auto-align: recover rotation/scale + warp overlay), multi-model (DINOv2 retrieve + LightGlue verify).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real ONNX graphs; every keypoint, correspondence, confidence, and RANSAC estimate is derived from real on-device runs. Multi-model verified: DINOv2 ranked the correct gallery image top (cos 0.876 same-scene vs 0.104 different) and LightGlue confirmed it with 736 strong correspondences.
rubric.notes: The similarity-transform estimate (wild) and the same-scene verdict (practical) are honest geometry/statistics on the model's real correspondences, clearly labelled as such (RANSAC), not a second model.

rubric.evidence: All buttons expose accessible names (buttons-have-names pass); sample/gallery images carry alt text (images-have-alt pass); the match canvas carries an aria-label describing the correspondence lines; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); sliders/selects/checkboxes are native labelled controls. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: The match-confidence histogram and per-pair/rank tables convey the same information as text, so the canvas is not the only channel.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid; the two-image pane grid collapses to one column <=560px; the match canvas scrolls inside its own overflow:auto container so a wide side-by-side never forces the page to scroll horizontally; sliders/readouts wrap. responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at desktop (1280x800) and narrow mobile (360x740, DPR3), light+dark.
rubric.notes: Mobile download cost (~51 MB; +~90 MB DINOv2 on the multi-model page) surfaced on the page. Tap targets meet ~44px on primary controls.

rubric.evidence: All inference AND the 640x640 resize preprocessing run off the main thread in a Web Worker; ImageBitmaps are transferred in (not cloned). The match overlay draw is a bounded set of lines/arcs on a canvas (not a per-pixel dense composite), redrawn only on run/slider change.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — extraction + matching never block the interface. Measured ~1.9 s extract + ~2.2 s match on WASM for a 1000+1000 keypoint pair.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, run heavy vision inference + preprocessing off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — both ONNX sessions AND the 640x640 resize run in a dedicated module Web Worker; ImageBitmaps are transferred in; the overlay draw is a bounded lines/arcs pass, not a per-pixel composite.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js + mm-worker.js off-thread + transfer([bitmaps]); UI responsive during matching; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive side-by-side image comparison without horizontal page overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; wide content should scroll inside its own container, not the page.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the image-pair grid collapses to one column on mobile; the wide match canvas lives in an overflow:auto wrapper so the page body never scrolls horizontally; controls/readouts wrap.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px page horizontal overflow measured by responsive-check on desktop+mobile, light+dark

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; label canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample/gallery images and controls carry accessible names; the match canvas has an aria-label.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas-rendered correspondences to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content and duplicate it in text.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the match canvas has an aria-label; the confidence histogram plus the numeric readouts (keypoints, matches, median confidence, inliers, recovered rotation/scale) convey the same data as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readouts/tables duplicate the visual

openQuestions: ALIKED requires a fixed 640x640 input; very wide/tall images are stretched before extraction. A letterbox variant (with un-letterbox mapping) could improve accuracy on extreme aspect ratios — worth measuring.
The wild page fits a similarity transform (scale+rotation+translation); a full homography (RANSAC + DLT) would handle perspective/planar scenes for true panorama warps — a candidate follow-up.
The multi-model page uses a small fixed gallery; scaling to a large gallery would want an approximate-nearest-neighbour index over the DINOv2 embeddings.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a homography (perspective) auto-align variant as a NEW wild demo for planar/panorama scenes, warping B onto A with a full 8-DOF transform.
Offer a letterbox preprocessing option for extreme aspect ratios, with the inverse mapping applied to keypoints, if measured to improve match quality.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for aliked-lightglue-matching (bukuroo/ALIKED-LightGlue-ONNX, keypoint-detection + learned matching, fp32, ~51 MB — ALIKED-N16rot detector/descriptor + LightGlue matcher; a DISTINCT two-image matching capability, not the single-image pose/landmark demos). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome via raw ONNX Runtime Web (transformers.js has no matcher class): standalone, an image vs itself shifted -50px yielded 925 matches with MEDIAN displacement -0.1565 = exactly -50/320 (real, geometrically-consistent correspondences); in-page the index matched a scene vs its rotated+scaled copy = 785 correspondences at median confidence 0.996, and the multi-model page's DINOv2 retrieval ranked the correct gallery image top (cos 0.876 vs 0.104) then LightGlue confirmed it with 736 strong matches. Off-main-thread worker (640x640 resize + both graphs + transferred bitmaps). Ladder: basics (match under a known transform), practical (same-scene/duplicate detector), wild (auto-align: recover rotation/scale + warp overlay), multi-model (DINOv2 retrieve + LightGlue verify). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. ALIKED BSD-3 + LightGlue Apache-2.0.
